STRUCTURAL.name: Structural fact
STRUCTURAL.note: Canonical, invariant fact (membership, charter, treaty) — the claim itself is the source. Used in place of a news/primary cite when no specific article is needed (e.g., 'Russia is a member of OPEC+').

BIS.name: US Bureau of Industry and Security
BIS.note: Administers US dual-use export controls (chips, tooling, EUV).

OPEC.name: OPEC Secretariat
OPEC.note: Authoritative source for OPEC/OPEC+ quotas, communiqués, membership.

STATE.name: US Department of State
STATE.note: Primary source for US treaty / framework agreements (EDCA, alliance arrangements).

CONGRESS_CRS.name: Congressional Research Service (Congress.gov)
CONGRESS_CRS.note: Primary-source CRS reports issued for Congress.

FED_DFA.name: Federal Reserve Distributional Financial Accounts
FED_DFA.note: Quarterly wealth-share by percentile. Source for the Top-1% / bottom-50% disparity indicator.

DOJ_EFTA.name: DOJ disclosures under the Epstein Files Transparency Act (H.R. 4405)
DOJ_EFTA.note: Primary-source document sets. Citable provenance (url + span) for the elite-incentive-network sub-graph (Lutnick / Mandelson / etc.) — once those actor / instrument nodes are added.

HOC_LIBRARY.name: UK House of Commons Library
HOC_LIBRARY.note: Briefing source for the 2026 Iran war / US-Iran ceasefire timeline.

SPANIEL.name: William Spaniel (Game Theory 101)
SPANIEL.note: Formal crisis-bargaining model of war. The three failure modes — private information / incentives to misrepresent, commitment problems, issue indivisibility — map directly onto edge annotations for why a conflict resists settlement.

WARFRONTS.name: Fronts / WarFronts (Simon Whistler)
WARFRONTS.note: Independent, audience-funded conflict/defense synthesis (ex-Warographics). Event-discovery and ground-truth layer; qualitative, self-declared bias-aware. Not to be confused with warfront.live.

FMP_BIGDATA.name: FMP via Bigdata.com market tearsheet
FMP_BIGDATA.note: Live multi-asset structural indicators (equities, commodities, yields, FX, crypto). Numeric, continuous, auto-updatable via connector — the highest-quality signal substrate.

AJ.name: Al Jazeera
AJ.note: International news with Middle East focus.

GLOBAL_TIMES.name: Global Times
GLOBAL_TIMES.note: Chinese state-affiliated outlet — useful as PRC framing signal, not unbiased reporting.

SCMP.name: South China Morning Post
SCMP.note: Hong Kong-based regional reporting on Greater China and Asia.

YAHOO_FINANCE.name: Yahoo Finance (public chart API)
YAHOO_FINANCE.note: Free unauthenticated quote / OHLC endpoint used by the refresh:indicators script.

seed:structural.name: Seed structural placeholder
seed:structural.note: Placeholder provenance key for hand-curated seed edges; replace with a real citation when backtesting or curating from source material.

FEC.name: Federal Election Commission filings
FEC.note: Primary campaign-finance disclosures used for bloc spending and donations.

HERITAGE.name: The Heritage Foundation
HERITAGE.note: Publisher of Project Esther and Project 2025; cited as the primary author of its own strategy documents.

JTA.name: Jewish Telegraphic Agency
JTA.note: Reporting on CUFI and other pro-Israel mobilization networks.

NYT.name: The New York Times
NYT.note: Reporting used for the Esther / policy-parallel note where causation is unconfirmed.

OPENSECRETS.name: OpenSecrets (CRP) — federal lobbying & election spending
OPENSECRETS.note: Aggregates LDA lobbying disclosures and FEC filings for bloc spending magnitudes.
